notes: Two studies: (1) a large-scale controlled framing experiment over 6 LLMs x 5 conditions x 497 CVE/patch files (14,910 queries) using the CrossVul dataset with quantitative detection-rate analysis plus manual justification/failure-mode categorization; (2) a controlled adversarial-attack evaluation of template-based vs. LLM-assisted iterative bias-injection attacks against 17 CVEs across 10 real-world ACR pipelines in an isolated environment, with a debiasing defense evaluation. 'Human subjects' refers to authors manually validating model outputs, not participant data, so human_subjects=false. Authors release a replication package (artifact/dataset contribution).
